# DIY Manual

Practical command cookbook for running X2Chess end-to-end from terminal cells (including Quarto).

Use this from repository root unless a section says otherwise.

## Quick Start (Most Used)

> Note: Browser mode is not used in this project. X2Chess runs as a Tauri desktop client.
> Note: X2Chess frontend uses port `5287`.
> Note: Developer-Tools defaults ON in `desktop:dev` and OFF in packaged PROD builds.
> Note: The Developer Tools panel (AST view) starts closed in all modes and must be opened via the app sidebar.

### 1) Check and remove stale dev processes (recommended before start)

```{bash}
# Run from repository root (workspace root)
echo "Before cleanup:"
lsof -i tcp:8011 || true
lsof -i tcp:5287 || true

# Graceful stop first
lsof -ti tcp:8011 | xargs -r kill -15
lsof -ti tcp:5287 | xargs -r kill -15
sleep 1

# If still running, force stop
lsof -ti tcp:8011 | xargs -r kill -9
lsof -ti tcp:5287 | xargs -r kill -9

echo "After cleanup:"
lsof -i tcp:8011 || true
lsof -i tcp:5287 || true
```

### 2) Set startup preferences mode (optional, DEV only)

In DEV mode the app can start with either factory defaults or your last saved
preferences.  A **dev prefs mode** flag stored in the app's localStorage
controls this.  The flag is initialised to `"defaults"` the very first time you
launch the app (no stored value → factory defaults).

Tip: To run JavaScript (e.g. `localStorage` commands): press **Cmd+Option+I** (macOS) or
**F12** to open the WebKit Web Inspector, then use its Console tab.
This keyboard shortcut is available in `desktop:dev` builds only.

Switch to **factory defaults** (useful for testing first-run behaviour):

```{js}
// Run in the Developer Dock console, then restart the app.
localStorage.setItem("x2chess.devPrefsMode", "defaults")
```

Switch to **your last saved preferences**:

```{js}
// Run in the Developer Dock console, then restart the app.
localStorage.setItem("x2chess.devPrefsMode", "user")
```

Check the current setting:

```{js}
localStorage.getItem("x2chess.devPrefsMode")   // "defaults" | "user" | null
```

> Note: This flag is only read in DEV builds.  PROD builds always restore saved
> user preferences and ignore this key.

### 3) Start Tauri desktop client (Terminal A)

Two launch modes are available.  Choose the one that fits your session:

**Isolated mode** — webview storage lives in `run/DEV/webview-data/` (under the repository root, separate from any
installed PROD build).  Recommended for normal development.

```{bash}
cd frontend
npm run desktop:dev:isolated
```

**Shared mode** — webview storage uses the OS default path (same location a packaged
PROD build of the same bundle ID would use).  Use this when you need to test against
real accumulated user data, or to verify a migration on an existing install.

```{bash}
cd frontend
npm run desktop:dev
```

> Note: Both scripts start their own frontend dev server on port `5287`.
> Note: `run/DEV/webview-data/` (under the repository root) is created automatically on first launch if it does not exist.
> Tip: To open the AST panel: open the app sidebar, enable the **Developer Tools** checkbox, then click **Open Developer Dock**.
> Tip: Use menu controls **Save mode** (`Autosave`/`Manual`) and **Save now** for the active game tab.

#### Resetting isolated dev storage

Delete the directory — the app recreates it with factory defaults on the next launch:

```{bash}
rm -rf run/DEV/webview-data
```

#### Using a custom storage path

Pass `X2CHESS_WEBVIEW_DATA` directly to redirect storage anywhere:

```{bash}
cd frontend
X2CHESS_WEBVIEW_DATA=/path/to/my/profile tauri dev
```

## Useful Scripts

### Install/update dependencies

```{bash}
cd frontend
npm install
```

### Build frontend for production

```{bash}
cd frontend
npm run build
```

### Run frontend unit tests

```{bash}
cd frontend
npm test
```

### Run regression tests (targeted)

```{bash}
cd frontend
node --test test/app_shell/dev_dock_regression.test.js
```

### Stop local dev services quickly

```{bash}
lsof -ti tcp:5287 | xargs -r kill -9
```
